page: Google Cloud Platform f1-micro is a burstable, shared-core virtual machine instance within the f1 family. It features 1 vCPU allocated from a shared physical Intel Xeon core on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 0.6 GB of system memory. The CPU operates with a single thread and includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 38 MB of L3 cache. It supports standard instruction set extensions such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES cryptography, and FMA. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place the f1-micro in the bottom 10% across all tested categories. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores are identical at 384.79, reflecting the single shared-core limitation. Memory bandwidth is highly constrained, with a read-only small block speed of 80.8 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 0.2 GB/sec. Passmark testing yields a CPU score of 236.08 and a memory score of 0.0. Database and transactional performance are similarly limited, with Redis SET operations reaching 0.24 million operations per second and static web serving throughput peaking at 0.22 GB/sec.

Due to its shared-core architecture and minimal memory footprint, the f1-micro represents a highly cost-efficient tier for low-intensity workloads where continuous CPU utilization is not required. The primary tradeoff is the potential for CPU throttling under sustained loads and severe memory constraints. This instance is best suited for lightweight utility tasks, simple cron jobs, basic development or testing environments, and low-traffic static web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform f1-micro is a shared-core virtual machine instance designed for low-intensity, budget-conscious workloads. It features a single vCPU on a shared physical Intel Xeon x86_64 core, 0.6 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark data indicates highly constrained performance, placing the instance in the bottom 10% across all metrics, including a stress-ng CPU score of 384.79, a Redis SET throughput of 0.24 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput of 0.22 GB/sec. The primary tradeoff is its limited memory capacity and shared CPU allocation, which can lead to throttling under sustained use. Consequently, the f1-micro is best suited for lightweight utility tasks, basic development environments, cron jobs, and low-traffic static web hosting where resource demands are minimal.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform f1-micro is a shared-core instance with 1 vCPU and 0.6 GB RAM. It is designed for low-intensity utility tasks, offering a budget-friendly but highly constrained hardware profile.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform f1-micro is a shared-core instance with 1 vCPU and 0.6 GB RAM, suited for lightweight utility tasks and basic testing.
tagline: A highly constrained, shared-core virtual machine designed for lightweight utility tasks, basic testing, and low-intensity background processes.

0.6 GB of system memory with no local storage or GPU
